prerequisites: An Ashby account with admin access. An Ashby Admin manages API keys and their endpoint permissions in the Ashby web app at https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/api/keys.
An Ashby API key (the connector's required `api_key` field). Ensure the key has permission for the endpoints this source reads.
A start date (the connector's required `start_date` field) marking the earliest data to sync.

authMethods.name: API key (HTTP Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: Ashby authenticates with an API key supplied via HTTP Basic authentication: send the API key as the Basic auth username and leave the password blank. Requests also require the header `Accept: application/json; version=1` and target the base URL https://api.ashbyhq.com. A missing key returns HTTP 401; an invalid, deactivated, or under-permissioned key returns HTTP 403. This maps to the connector's `api_key` secret field.

setupSteps.title: Obtain and authorize an Ashby API key
setupSteps.body: Have an Ashby Admin open the API keys page at https://app.ashbyhq.com/admin/api/keys and create (or select) an API key. The Admin can manage the permissions for existing API keys there, so confirm the key is granted access to the endpoints this source needs. A key that lacks the required endpoint permissions will be rejected with HTTP 403.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the API key in the connector's required `api_key` field. The connector sends it using HTTP Basic auth (API key as username, blank password) against https://api.ashbyhq.com with the `Accept: application/json; version=1` header.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required `start_date` field to the earliest date from which the connector should sync data. Records before this date are not retrieved.
